title: Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua
what_description: Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the family Rosaceae. It is a deciduous shrub with erect, thorny stems, and pinnate, dark green leaves, consisting of 3-5 leaflets, 1-3 cm long. The leaves have serrated margins and are glandular-dotted. The plant produces fragrant, pink to purple flowers, 4-6 cm in diameter, with 5 petals, in clusters at the ends of the branches. The flowers are followed by red, globose hips, 1-2 cm in diameter, containing numerous achenes.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua are inflammation, wound healing, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to stimulate blood flow. Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua has been shown to treat fungal infections by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and promoting the production of antifungal compounds. Diarrhea and digestive issues can be alleviated by the plant's ability to regulate gut motility and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Skin irritation and skin infections can be treated with the plant's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, while cough and respiratory issues can be alleviated by its expectorant and bronchodilator effects. Additionally, Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua has been found to be effective in treating wounds, rheumatism, insect bites, anxiety, stress, hypertension, acne, bacterial infections, and gastritis.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua are its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. This plant also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, primarily due to the saponins and triterpenoids it contains, which help alleviate inflammation and reduce pain, thereby contributing to its analgesic effects. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua can be attributed to its volatile oils, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, making it useful in the treatment of various infections. Furthermore, the cardioprotective effects of this plant are thought to be mediated by its ability to scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can lead to cardiovascular disease. The bioactive compounds responsible for these properties include flavonoids, saponins, volatile oils, and phenolic acids.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua are rosmarinic acid, quercetin, gallic acid, kaempferol, and ellagic acid, which have been identified through various phytochemical studies. Rosmarinic acid possesses antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, attributed to its ability to scavenge free radicals and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Quercetin and gallic acid exhibit potent antioxidant activities, with quercetin also exhibiting anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective effects, while gallic acid has shown antimicrobial and anti-viral properties. Kaempferol has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and anti-cardiovascular disease properties, attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and induce apoptosis in cancer cells. Ellagic acid has been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties, with potential applications in the prevention and treatment of various diseases, including cancer and cardiovascular disease.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua are rosehip oil extracted from its seeds, rich in essential fatty acids, vitamins, and antioxidants, used in skincare products for anti-aging and wound healing properties, rose petal tea made from its fragrant flowers, consumed as a calming and antispasmodic agent, rosewater obtained through steam distillation, used in perfumes, aromatherapy, and as a skin toner due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, rosehip tea prepared from its fruit, utilized as a remedy for digestive issues and as a natural laxative, and rose syrup produced from its flowers, utilized as a flavoring agent and a potential treatment for sore throats and coughs.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua improperly include inducing nausea, causing diarrhea, triggering allergic reactions, interacting with medications, and exacerbating allergies. This is due to the presence of compounds like terpenes, flavonoids, and fragrances that can be toxic in high concentrations. Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua contains volatile oils that can cause gastrointestinal upset, skin irritation, and respiratory issues in individuals with sensitivities or allergies. Additionally, the interaction with medications may be caused by the inhibition of certain enzymes, leading to decreased efficacy or increased toxicity of prescribed drugs. Ingesting Rosa dumalis subsp. innocua can also worsen pre-existing allergies, particularly those related to pollen or plant-derived substances.
